mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, systematically reducing S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production during early expansion phases indicates improving manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, boosting short-term market sentiment.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >50% of early-cycle observations, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 205.0 triggers market expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months, or the threshold shows no incremental predictive value over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust job creation without labor market overheating, supporting positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenues, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.03 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100.0 baseline index signals a contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or it fails to outperform a naive benchmark in out-of-sample tests.
